1974.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Whoever Allah intends good for, He grants him understanding of the religion."
1974.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang Allah kehendaki kebaikan baginya, niscaya Allah akan pahamkan dia dalam urusan agama."

1975.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Good health and free time are two blessings of Allah of which many people are heedless."
1975.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Kesehatan dan waktu luang adalah dua nikmat dari nikmat-nikmat Allah yang banyak manusia terlalaikan."

1976.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Whoever eavesdrops on a people who dislike being heard will have molten lead poured into his ears."
1976.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang menguping pembicaraan suatu kaum yang tidak suka didengar, maka akan dituangkan timah panas ke telinganya."

1977.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Do not follow the first glance with another, for the first is forgiven but the second is not."
1977.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ali ibn Abi Talib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Janganlah kamu mengikuti pandangan pertama dengan pandangan kedua, karena pandangan pertama dimaafkan sedangkan yang kedua tidak."

1978.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sufyan ibn Abdullah al-Thaqafi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Prophet ☯ about Islam and was told: 'Fear Allah, then be steadfast.' When he asked what he feared most for him, the Prophet ☯ pointed to his tongue.
1978.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sufyan ibn Abdullah al-Thaqafi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia bertanya kepada Nabi ☯ tentang Islam dan dijawab: 'Bertakwalah kepada Allah, kemudian istiqamahlah.' Ketika ditanya apa yang paling dikhawatirkan atas dirinya, Nabi ☯ menunjuk pada lisannya.

1979.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sufyan ibn Abdullah al-Thaqafi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Prophet ☯ what he feared most for him, whereupon the Prophet ☯ took hold of his own tongue and said: 'This.'
1979.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sufyan ibn Abdullah al-Thaqafi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia bertanya kepada Nabi ☯ tentang apa yang paling dikhawatirkan atas dirinya, maka Nabi ☯ memegang lidahnya sendiri dan bersabda: 'Ini.'

1980.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) that it was asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, which aspect of Islam is best?' He said: 'The one from whose tongue and hand the Muslims are safe.'
1980.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ditanyakan: 'Wahai Rasulullah, Islam manakah yang paling utama?' Beliau menjawab: 'Orang yang kaum muslimin selamat dari lisan dan tangannya.'

1981.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Whoever stays silent is saved."
1981.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang diam, maka selamatlah dia."

1982.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked about backbiting. He said: 'Mentioning your brother in a way he dislikes.' It was asked: 'What if what I say about him is true?' He said: 'If it is true, you have backbitten him; if it is false, you have slandered him.'
1982.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia ditanya tentang ghibah. Beliau menjawab: 'Engkau menyebut saudaramu dengan sesuatu yang tidak disukainya.' Ditanyakan: 'Bagaimana jika apa yang aku katakan itu benar?' Beliau menjawab: 'Jika benar, maka engkau telah mengghibahnya; jika tidak benar, maka engkau telah memfitnahnya.'

1983.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Truthfulness leads to righteousness and righteousness leads to Paradise. A man keeps telling the truth until he is recorded as a truthful person. Lying leads to wickedness and wickedness leads to the Fire. A man keeps lying until he is recorded as a liar."
1983.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Kejujuran membawa kepada kebaikan dan kebaikan membawa kepada surga. Seseorang terus berkata jujur hingga dicatat sebagai orang yang jujur. Kedustaan membawa kepada kefasikan dan kefasikan membawa kepada neraka. Seseorang terus berdusta hingga dicatat sebagai pendusta."

1984.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "The Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hand the Muslims are safe."
1984.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Muslim sejati adalah orang yang kaum muslimin selamat dari lisan dan tangannya."

1985.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ mentioned a man who journeys far, disheveled and dusty, raising his hands to the sky saying 'O Lord! O Lord!' while his food is unlawful, his drink is unlawful, his clothing is unlawful, and he has been nourished on unlawful things — so how can his supplication be answered?
1985.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ menyebut seorang laki-laki yang menempuh perjalanan jauh, lusuh dan berdebu, mengangkat tangannya ke langit seraya berkata 'Ya Rabb! Ya Rabb!' sementara makanannya haram, minumannya haram, pakaiannya haram, dan dia dikenyangkan dengan yang haram — maka bagaimana mungkin doanya dikabulkan?

1986.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "It is sufficient for one of you from this world to have a servant and a mount."
1986.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah al-Aslami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Cukuplah bagi salah seorang dari kalian dari dunia ini seorang pembantu dan seekor kendaraan."

1987.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mirdas al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "The righteous will depart one after another, and the dregs will remain like the worthless residue of barley or dates; Allah will not care about them at all."
1987.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mirdas al-Aslami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Orang-orang saleh akan pergi satu demi satu, dan yang tersisa hanyalah ampas seperti ampas jelai atau kurma; Allah tidak akan peduli sedikit pun terhadap mereka."

1988.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Many a person who fasts gains nothing from his fasting but thirst, and many a person who prays at night gains nothing from his night prayer but sleeplessness."
1988.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Betapa banyak orang yang berpuasa tidak mendapatkan apa pun dari puasanya selain rasa haus, dan betapa banyak orang yang shalat malam tidak mendapatkan apa pun dari shalatnya selain begadang."

1989.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ mentioned the prayer one day and said: 'Whoever maintains the prayer, it will be a light, a proof, and a means of salvation for him on the Day of Judgment. Whoever does not maintain it will have no light, no proof, and no salvation, and will be raised with Pharaoh, Haman, Qarun, and Ubayy ibn Khalaf.'
1989.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ menyebut shalat pada suatu hari dan bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang menjaga shalat, maka shalat itu akan menjadi cahaya, bukti, dan keselamatan baginya pada hari Kiamat. Barangsiapa yang tidak menjaganya, maka tidak akan memiliki cahaya, bukti, dan keselamatan, dan akan dibangkitkan bersama Fir'aun, Haman, Qarun, dan Ubay ibn Khalaf.'

1990.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ used to encourage night prayer and said: 'Even if it is just one rak'ah.'
1990.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ senantiasa mendorong untuk melaksanakan shalat malam dan bersabda: 'Walau hanya satu rakaat.'

1991.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ replied to his question: 'Where are you with seeking forgiveness? Indeed, I seek Allah's forgiveness one hundred times in a day.'
1991.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari a companion (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ menjawab pertanyaannya: 'Di mana kamu dengan istighfar? Sesungguhnya aku beristighfar kepada Allah seratus kali dalam sehari.'

1992.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ said: 'I seek Allah's forgiveness and turn to Him in repentance more than seventy times each day.'
1992.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Musa al-Ash'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ bersabda: 'Aku memohon ampun kepada Allah dan bertobat kepada-Nya lebih dari tujuh puluh kali setiap hari.'

1993.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ recited: 'He is worthy of being feared and worthy of forgiving' (al-Muddaththir: 56), and said: 'Your Lord says: I am worthy of being feared, so whoever fears Me, I am worthy of forgiving him.'
1993.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ membacakan: 'Dia-lah yang berhak ditakuti dan berhak memberi ampunan' (al-Muddatstsir: 56), lalu bersabda: 'Rabb kalian berfirman: Aku berhak untuk ditakuti, maka barangsiapa yang takut kepada-Ku, Aku berhak untuk mengampuninya.'

1994.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "I know a verse that, if people acted upon it, it would suffice them — and he recited: 'And whoever fears Allah, He will make for him a way out.' (al-Talaq: 2)."
1994.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Aku mengetahui sebuah ayat yang jika manusia mengamalkannya niscaya akan mencukupi mereka — dan beliau membacakan: 'Dan barangsiapa yang bertakwa kepada Allah, niscaya Allah akan memberikan kepadanya jalan keluar.' (al-Thalaq: 2)."

1995.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "O Aisha, beware of minor sins, for they have a witness from Allah."
1995.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Wahai Aisyah, berhati-hatilah terhadap dosa-dosa kecil, karena sesungguhnya ada yang menyaksikannya dari Allah."

1996.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Every son of Adam commits sins, and the best of those who commit sins are those who repent."
1996.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Setiap anak Adam pasti berdosa, dan sebaik-baik orang yang berdosa adalah yang bertobat."

1997.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ gave the parable of a man whose camel strayed on a journey. He searched in despair, then lay under a tree awaiting death — then found his camel returned with his provisions. His joy is like the joy of Allah when a servant repents.
1997.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ membuat perumpamaan tentang seorang laki-laki yang kendaraannya lepas dalam perjalanan. Dia mencari-carinya dengan putus asa, lalu berbaring di bawah pohon menunggu kematian — kemudian kendaraannya kembali membawa bekalnya. Kebahagiaannya itu seperti kebahagiaan Allah ketika seorang hamba bertobat.

1998.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ drew a square, a line through the middle, lines around it, and a line extending outside, saying: 'This is man; this square is his appointed term; these lines are the misfortunes that afflict him — if one misses him, another strikes him; and this external line is his hope.'
1998.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ menggambar sebuah persegi, garis di tengahnya, garis-garis di sekelilingnya, dan sebuah garis yang keluar, seraya bersabda: 'Ini adalah manusia; persegi ini adalah ajalnya; garis-garis ini adalah musibah-musibah yang menimpanya — jika satu luput, yang lain mengenainya; dan garis yang keluar ini adalah angan-angannya.'

1999.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ka'b ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Two hungry wolves unleashed upon a flock of sheep are not more destructive than a person's greed for wealth and status is to his religion."
1999.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ka'b ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Dua serigala lapar yang dilepas ke dalam sekumpulan domba tidaklah lebih merusak dibandingkan kerakusan seseorang terhadap harta dan kedudukan terhadap agamanya."

2000.iwh_en: It was narrated from Wathilah ibn al-Asqa' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ narrated from his Lord: 'I am as My servant thinks of Me, so let him think of Me as he wishes.'
2000.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Wathilah ibn al-Asqa' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ meriwayatkan dari Rabb-nya: 'Aku sesuai dengan prasangka hamba-Ku terhadap-Ku, maka hendaklah dia berprasangka kepada-Ku sesuai yang dia kehendaki.'

2001.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that when Allah revealed the verse about warning close kindred, the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: 'O people of Quraysh, ransom yourselves from Allah; I cannot avail you anything against Allah. O Fatimah, daughter of Muhammad, ask me what you will of my wealth — I cannot avail you anything against Allah.'
2001.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Allah menurunkan ayat tentang memberi peringatan kepada kerabat dekat, Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: 'Wahai orang-orang Quraisy, tebuslah diri kalian dari Allah; aku tidak dapat membela kalian sedikit pun dari Allah. Wahai Fatimah putri Muhammad, mintalah kepadaku apa yang kamu mau dari hartaku — aku tidak dapat membelamu dari Allah.'

2002.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: 'Draw near and be steadfast, and know that none of you will be saved by his deeds.' They asked: 'Not even you?' He said: 'Not even I, unless Allah envelops me in His mercy.'
2002.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: 'Mendekatlah dan luruskanlah, dan ketahuilah bahwa tidak seorang pun dari kalian yang akan diselamatkan oleh amalnya.' Mereka bertanya: 'Tidak juga engkau?' Beliau menjawab: 'Tidak juga aku, kecuali Allah melimpahkan rahmat-Nya kepadaku.'

2003.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ said: 'Every one of you has a companion from the jinn and a companion from the angels.' They asked: 'And you, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'Yes, but Allah helped me against mine so he became Muslim and only commands good.'
2003.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ bersabda: 'Setiap orang dari kalian memiliki pendamping dari jin dan pendamping dari malaikat.' Mereka bertanya: 'Dan engkau, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau menjawab: 'Ya, tetapi Allah membantuku terhadapnya sehingga dia masuk Islam dan hanya memerintahkan kebaikan.'

2004.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "If you knew what I know, you would laugh little and weep much."
2004.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Seandainya kalian mengetahui apa yang aku ketahui, niscaya kalian akan sedikit tertawa dan banyak menangis."

2005.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ passed by a dead, scrawny sheep and said: 'Do you think this is worthless to its owner?' They said: 'Yes.' He said: 'By Allah, this world is more worthless to Allah than this sheep is to its owner.'
2005.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ melewati bangkai kambing yang kurus dan bersabda: 'Apakah menurut kalian ini tidak berharga bagi pemiliknya?' Mereka menjawab: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Demi Allah, dunia ini lebih tidak berharga di sisi Allah daripada kambing ini di sisi pemiliknya.'

2006.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked the Prophet ☯: 'Which deeds are best?' He said: 'Faith in Allah and jihad in the path of Allah.'
2006.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dharr al-Ghifari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya kepada Nabi ☯: 'Amal apakah yang paling utama?' Beliau menjawab: 'Iman kepada Allah dan jihad di jalan Allah.'

2007.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ja'far al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ said: 'The best deed with Allah is faith without any doubt.'
2007.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ja'far al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ bersabda: 'Amal yang paling utama di sisi Allah adalah iman yang tidak ada keraguan di dalamnya.'

2008.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself."
2008.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Tidak beriman salah seorang dari kalian hingga dia mencintai untuk saudaranya apa yang dia cintai untuk dirinya sendiri."

2009.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "None of you truly believes until I am more beloved to him than his parent, his child, and all of mankind."
2009.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Tidak beriman salah seorang dari kalian hingga aku lebih dia cintai daripada orang tuanya, anaknya, dan seluruh manusia."

2010.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Busr (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked: 'Which people are best?' He said: 'The one whose life is long and whose deeds are good.' He asked: 'Which people are worst?' He said: 'The one whose life is long and whose deeds are bad.'
2010.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Busr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya: 'Manusia manakah yang terbaik?' Beliau menjawab: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan baik amalnya.' Dia bertanya: 'Manusia manakah yang terburuk?' Beliau menjawab: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan buruk amalnya.'

2011.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Is there anyone better than us? We embraced Islam with you and fought with you.' The Prophet ☯ said: 'Yes — people who will come after you, who will believe in me without having seen me.'
2011.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Adakah orang yang lebih baik dari kami? Kami masuk Islam bersamamu dan berjihad bersamamu.' Nabi ☯ menjawab: 'Ya — suatu kaum yang akan datang setelahmu, mereka beriman kepadaku tanpa pernah melihatku.'

2012.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "It is bad for one of you to say he forgot such and such a verse — rather he was made to forget. So review the Quran, for it slips from the hearts faster than camels from their tethers."
2012.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Sungguh buruk bagi salah seorang dari kalian mengatakan dia lupa ayat ini dan itu — bahkan dia telah dilupakan. Maka ulangilah membaca al-Quran, karena ia lebih cepat lepas dari hati daripada terlepasnya unta dari ikatannya."

2013.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Masud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "None of you should say he is better than Yunus ibn Matta."
2013.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Masud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian mengatakan bahwa dia lebih baik dari Yunus ibn Matta."

2014.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ described the forms of charity available to all people: using one's physical strength, helping the one in distress, commanding good, and refraining from evil — all count as charity.
2014.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ menggambarkan bentuk-bentuk sedekah yang tersedia bagi semua orang: menggunakan tenaga fisik, menolong orang yang dalam kesulitan, memerintahkan kebaikan, dan menahan diri dari keburukan — semuanya terhitung sebagai sedekah.

2015.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hind al-Dari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Messenger of Allah ☯ say: 'Whoever stands up for the truth when it is difficult — upon him be blessings.'
2015.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hind al-Dari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia mendengar Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang menegakkan kebenaran ketika itu sulit — semoga keberkahan tercurah atasnya.'

2016.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ said: 'The parable of the believer is like a green plant: the wind bends it and then it stands upright again, and he is afflicted with trials until death. The parable of the hypocrite is like a solid cedar tree: nothing touches it until it is uprooted all at once.'
2016.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ bersabda: 'Perumpamaan seorang mukmin adalah seperti tanaman hijau yang ditiup angin hingga membungkuk lalu tegak kembali, dan dia ditimpa cobaan-cobaan hingga kematian datang. Perumpamaan orang munafik adalah seperti pohon araz yang kokoh: tidak ada sesuatu pun yang menerpanya hingga ia tumbang sekaligus.'

2017.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hakim ibn Hizam (may Allah be pleased with him) that he asked the Messenger of Allah ☯ for something and he gave him, then he asked again and he gave, then asked again and he gave, then asked again, so the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: 'O Hakim, this wealth is green and sweet. Whoever takes it with a generous soul will be blessed in it, and whoever takes it with a craving soul will not be blessed — like one who eats but is never satisfied. The upper hand is better than the lower hand.'
2017.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hakim ibn Hizam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia meminta kepada Rasulullah ☯ sesuatu dan beliau memberinya, lalu meminta lagi dan beliau memberi, lalu meminta lagi dan beliau memberi, lalu meminta lagi, maka Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: 'Wahai Hakim, harta ini hijau lagi manis. Barangsiapa mengambilnya dengan jiwa yang lapang, dia akan diberkahi, dan barangsiapa mengambilnya dengan jiwa yang rakus, tidak akan diberkahi — seperti orang yang makan tapi tidak pernah kenyang. Tangan yang di atas lebih baik dari tangan yang di bawah.'

2018.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ forbade burying daughters alive, disobeying mothers, withholding what is due, taking what does not belong, and asking for a girl's hand in marriage without fulfilling the commitment.
2018.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ melarang mengubur anak perempuan hidup-hidup, durhaka kepada ibu, menahan hak orang lain dan mengambil yang bukan haknya, serta meminang wanita tanpa menepati komitmen.

2019.iwh_en: It was narrated from Thawban (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: 'What I fear most for my community is misguiding leaders.'
2019.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Thawban (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya yang paling aku takutkan atas umatku adalah para pemimpin yang menyesatkan.'

2020.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: 'Let a man support his brother whether he is the oppressor or the oppressed. If he is the oppressor, let him stop him — that is his support. If he is the oppressed, let him help him.'
2020.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: 'Hendaklah seseorang menolong saudaranya baik ketika dia berbuat zalim maupun dizalimi. Jika dia berbuat zalim, hendaklah dia mencegahnya — itulah pertolongan untuknya. Jika dia dizalimi, hendaklah dia menolongnya.'

2021.iwh_en: It was narrated from Tamim al-Dari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ☯ said: "Religion is sincere advice. We asked: For whom, O Messenger of Allah? He said: For Allah, His Messenger, His Book, the leaders of the Muslims, and their common people."
2021.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Tamim al-Dari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ☯ bersabda: "Agama adalah nasihat. Kami bertanya: Untuk siapa, wahai Rasulullah? Beliau menjawab: Untuk Allah, Rasul-Nya, Kitab-Nya, para pemimpin kaum muslimin, dan kaum muslimin pada umumnya."

2022.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ☯ said: 'Islam began as something strange and will return to being strange — so blessed are the strangers.' It was asked: 'Who are the strangers?' He said: 'Those who break away from their tribes.'
2022.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ☯ bersabda: 'Islam dimulai dalam keadaan asing dan akan kembali menjadi asing — maka beruntunglah orang-orang yang asing itu.' Ditanyakan: 'Siapakah orang-orang yang asing itu?' Beliau menjawab: 'Mereka yang memisahkan diri dari kabilah-kabilah mereka.'

2023.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ☯ said: 'Whoever loves to meet Allah, Allah loves to meet him; whoever hates to meet Allah, Allah hates to meet him.' Aisha said: 'But we dislike death.' He said: 'It is not that — when a believer is given glad tidings of Allah's mercy at the moment of death, he loves to meet Allah, and Allah loves to meet him.'
2023.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ☯ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang mencintai pertemuan dengan Allah, maka Allah mencintai pertemuan dengannya; barangsiapa yang membenci pertemuan dengan Allah, maka Allah membenci pertemuan dengannya.' Aisyah berkata: 'Tapi kami tidak menyukai kematian.' Beliau bersabda: 'Bukan itu maksudnya — ketika seorang mukmin diberi kabar gembira tentang rahmat Allah pada saat kematian, dia mencintai pertemuan dengan Allah, maka Allah pun mencintai pertemuan dengannya.'
